message:
feat(search): add convenience flags for two-tier search and fix quiet mode

CLI improvements for two-tier progressive search:

cli.rs:
- Add --fast-only flag: Two-tier with blend_factor=0.0 (hash-only, fastest)
- Add --quality-only flag: Two-tier with blend_factor=1.0 (best quality)
- Add --blend-factor <0.0-1.0> flag: Custom fast/quality blend ratio
- Flags are mutually exclusive and imply --mode two-tier
- Add validate_blend_factor() for range validation

output.rs:
- Add print_data() method that bypasses quiet mode suppression
- Data output (JSON, CSV, TOON) should never be suppressed by --quiet
- Quiet mode should only affect diagnostic/progress messages, not results

main.rs:
- Compute effective_mode from CLI flags (fast_only/quality_only/blend_factor)
- Override config blend_factor based on CLI flags
- Replace output.print() with output.print_data() for all data output:
  - Search results (JSON, TOON)
  - DM context output
  - Stats output
  - Tweet/thread output
  - Doctor output
  - Robot docs output
- Ensures xf search -q still outputs results while suppressing progress

This enables simpler CLI usage:
  xf search "query" --fast-only    # Instant hash-based results
  xf search "query" --quality-only # Wait for quality embeddings
  xf search "query" --blend-factor 0.3  # Custom blend

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>

files.patch:
@@ -271,6 +271,27 @@ pub struct SearchArgs {
     /// Force direct inference (no daemon)
     #[arg(long, conflicts_with = "daemon")]
     pub no_daemon: bool,
+
+    /// Two-tier search: use only fast (hash) results, skip quality refinement.
+    ///
+    /// Equivalent to --mode two-tier with blend_factor=0.0.
+    /// Fastest option, uses only hash-based similarity.
+    #[arg(long, conflicts_with_all = ["quality_only", "blend_factor"])]
+    pub fast_only: bool,
+
+    /// Two-tier search: wait for quality results only, skip fast phase display.
+    ///
+    /// Equivalent to --mode two-tier with blend_factor=1.0.
+    /// Highest quality, but no instant results.
+    #[arg(long, conflicts_with_all = ["fast_only", "blend_factor"])]
+    pub quality_only: bool,
+
+    /// Two-tier search: blend factor for combining fast and quality results.
+    ///
+    /// Range: 0.0 (fast-only) to 1.0 (quality-only). Default: 0.7.
+    /// Implies --mode two-tier if not already set.
+    #[arg(long, value_parser = validate_blend_factor, conflicts_with_all = ["fast_only", "quality_only"])]
+    pub blend_factor: Option<f32>,
 }
 
 #[derive(Args, Debug)]
@@ -536,6 +557,17 @@ fn validate_mrl_dims(s: &str) -> Result<usize, String> {
     Ok(dims)
 }
 
+/// Validate blend factor (must be between 0.0 and 1.0).
+fn validate_blend_factor(s: &str) -> Result<f32, String> {
+    let factor: f32 = s
+        .parse()
+        .map_err(|_| format!("invalid blend factor: {s}"))?;
+    if !(0.0..=1.0).contains(&factor) {
+        return Err("blend factor must be between 0.0 and 1.0".to_string());
+    }
+    Ok(factor)
+}
+
 #[derive(ValueEnum, Clone, Debug, PartialEq, Eq)]
 pub enum DataType {
     Tweet,
